sections.subtitle: Match Overview and Stakes
sections.text: Roma, sitting at 7th in the league with 60 points, host Fiorentina, who are just one spot and one point behind at 8th with 59 points, in a crucial Serie A matchup on May 4, 2025, at 16:00 at the Stadio Olimpico in Rome. This Round 35 clash could have significant implications for European qualification spots as both teams are neck and neck in the standings. With only a point separating them, a win for either side could provide a psychological and positional edge heading into the final stretch of the season. The atmosphere in Rome is expected to be electric as both teams vie for crucial points in this tightly contested battle.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between Roma and Fiorentina, the results are evenly split with 1 win for Roma, 2 draws, and 2 wins for Fiorentina. Their most recent encounter on October 27, 2024, saw Fiorentina dominate Roma with a resounding 5-1 victory at home, where Edoardo Bove earned the Player of the Match title with an impressive Sofascore rating of 8.6. Head-to-head streaks show Fiorentina have gone 4 matches without a loss against Roma, while both teams have struggled to keep clean sheets, with Roma failing to do so in their last 4 home games against Fiorentina and Fiorentina in their last 5 away games against Roma. Additionally, both teams have scored in 4 of their last 5 encounters, suggesting we might see goals at both ends again.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Roma come into this match with a solid streak of 6 games without a loss, showing resilience in their recent performances. However, they’ve managed fewer than 2.5 goals in their last 6 home games, indicating a potential struggle to break down defenses at the Stadio Olimpico. On the other hand, Fiorentina have not kept a clean sheet in their last 4 away games, conceding consistently on the road, and their matches have seen over 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 7 away outings. Both teams scoring has been a recurring theme for Fiorentina, happening in 6 of their last 7 away games, which could hint at an open game. Card counts also differ, with Fiorentina seeing over 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 7 away matches, while Roma have had under 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 6 home games.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Roma
sections.text: For Roma, Paulo Dybala stands out as a key figure with a league rank of 2 in average rating at 7.39, and he’s also fouled 2.5 times per game, ranking 5th in the league, making him a constant threat and a likely target for Fiorentina’s defenders. Artem Dovbyk, Roma’s forward, has scored 11 goals against an expected goals figure of 12.01, showing clinical finishing, while Alexis Saelemaekers has overperformed his expected goals of 1.89 by scoring 6, with a strong rating of 7.23. Matías Soulé is another name to watch, contributing 5 goals and 2 assists with a rating of 7.17, adding flair to Roma’s attack. Meanwhile, Bryan Cristante in midfield provides stability with an average rating of 7.0, chipping in with 2 goals and 2 assists.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Fiorentina
sections.text: Fiorentina’s attacking threat is led by Moise Kean, who has scored an impressive 17 goals this season, ranking 2nd in the league, against an expected goals tally of 15.86, showcasing his lethal finishing. Yacine Adli, with a rating of 7.07, has been a standout in midfield, ranking 4th in the league for percentage of long balls at 9.4%, and contributing 4 goals and 5 assists, including strong performances against Roma in past matchups with an average rating of 7.7 over the last 5 games. Albert Guðmundsson adds depth to the attack with 6 goals, while Robin Gosens has been impactful from defense, scoring 5 goals and providing 4 assists with a rating of 7.08. Rolando Mandragora, in form over the last 5 matches with a rating of 7.36, has also netted 2 goals and 2 assists recently.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistics
sections.text: Roma have scored 49 goals this season, ranking 9th in the league, and conceded only 32, placing them 4th for least goals conceded, showing a strong defensive setup. They excel in ball possession with 54.65% (6th in league), accurate passes at 85.94% (6th), and successful final third passes per game at 95.26 (4th), indicating a controlled, possession-based style. Fiorentina, while specific stats aren’t fully detailed, show a tendency to score early in matches, with 29.41% of their goals coming between minutes 46-60 and 26.47% between 16-30. Roma, conversely, are dangerous late, scoring 21.88% of their goals between minutes 61-75 and 18.75% in the final 15 minutes. Roma’s 14 clean sheets (4th in league) contrast with Fiorentina’s defensive vulnerabilities on the road, setting up an intriguing tactical battle.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Trends
sections.text: The referee for this match, Daniele Chiffi, averages 4.3 yellow cards per game and 0.24 red cards per game, which aligns with the head-to-head trend of over 4.5 cards in all of the last 5 encounters between these teams. Roma have accumulated 59 yellow cards this season (6th least in the league) with an average of 1.74 per game, while Fiorentina’s away games often see high card counts. In terms of set-pieces, Roma average 4.44 corners per game (12th in league), and head-to-head stats show under 10.5 corners in 6 of their last 8 meetings, suggesting a game not overly dominated by corner opportunities. Roma’s 16 woodwork hits (3rd unluckiest in the league) also hint at near-misses that could turn into goals on their day.

sections.subtitle: Injuries and Absences
sections.text: Fiorentina face a challenging situation with several missing players, including key midfielder Danilo Cataldi, who has scored 3 goals this season, and Edoardo Bove, who was the Player of the Match in their last meeting against Roma with a rating of 8.6. Other absences include defenders Dodô and Luca Ranieri, as well as Andrea Colpani, which could weaken Fiorentina’s squad depth and tactical flexibility. Roma, on the other hand, report no missing players from the provided data, giving them a potential edge with a full-strength lineup to choose from. These absences could impact Fiorentina’s ability to maintain their recent form against Roma.
